#REDIRECT [[温泉]]

'''硫酸塩泉'''（りゅうさんえんせん）は、掲示用泉質名に基づく[[温泉]]の[[泉質]]の分類の一種。
== 概要 == [[アルカリ金属]]・[[アルカリ土類金属]]の[[硫酸塩]]を主成分としていることからこの名称が付いている。俗に薬効が高いと言われる。
== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。
=== 旧泉質名 === * 硫酸塩泉 ** 純硫酸塩泉 ** 正苦味泉 ** 芒硝泉 ** 石膏泉 ** 含食塩 - 芒硝泉 ** 含食塩 - 石膏泉 ** 含食塩 - 正苦味泉
=== 新泉質名 === * 硫酸塩泉 ** マグネシウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** ナトリウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** カルシウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉 ** カルシウム・ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉 ** マグネシウム・ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]のもの。
== 効能 == 泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 * 外傷、痛風、肩こり、腰痛、神経痛など ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保障するものではない''
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[四万温泉]]
== 関連項目 == {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}} *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[外湯]]、[[温泉番付]] *[[日本の温泉地一覧]] - [[日本の温泉画像一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉]]

'''硫酸塩泉'''（りゅうさんえんせん）は、掲示用泉質名に基づく[[温泉]]の[[泉質]]の分類の一種。
== 概要 == [[アルカリ金属]]・[[アルカリ土類金属]]の[[硫酸塩]]を主成分としていることからこの名称が付いている。俗に薬効が高いと言われる。
== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。
=== 旧泉質名 === * 硫酸塩泉 ** 純硫酸塩泉 ** 正苦味泉 ** 芒硝泉 ** 石膏泉 ** 含食塩 - 芒硝泉 ** 含食塩 - 石膏泉 ** 含食塩 - 正苦味泉
=== 新泉質名 === * 硫酸塩泉 ** 硫酸塩泉 ** マグネシウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** ナトリウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** カルシウム - 硫酸塩泉 ** ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉 ** カルシウム・ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉 ** マグネシウム・ナトリウム - 硫酸塩・塩化物泉
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保障するものではない'' 泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。
=== 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、動脈硬化症、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては、[[下痢]]のとき。 * ナトリウム - 硫酸塩泉は、[[腎臓病]]、[[高血圧症]]、むくみがあるとき、[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のとき[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 関連項目 == {{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉]] [[Category:硫酸塩|*りゆうさんえんせん]]

泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。
=== 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、動脈硬化症、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]

{| class="wikitable" |+ 新旧泉質名の対応<ref>{{Cite web| |title=参考資料２新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! scope="col" id="旧泉質名" | 旧泉質名 ! scope="col" id="新泉質名" | 新泉質名 ! scope="col" id="略記泉質名" | 略記泉質名 |- style="background-color:#eef" | 硫酸塩泉 || 硫酸塩泉 || SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- | 純硫酸塩泉 || 硫酸塩泉 || SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- | 正苦味泉 || マグネシウム－硫酸塩泉 || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- | 芒硝泉 || ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉 || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- | 石膏泉 || カルシウム－硫酸塩泉 || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- | 含食塩－芒硝泉 || ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉 || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- | 含食塩－石膏泉 || カルシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉 || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- | 含食塩－正苦味泉 || マグネシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉 || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |}
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン#電荷による種類|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]] (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。
=== 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム - 硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧症]]、[[浮腫|むくみ]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。

== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。 {| class="wikitable" |+ 新旧泉質名の対応<ref>{{Cite web| |title=参考資料2新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> !旧泉質名!!新泉質名!!略記泉質名 |- |colspan="2" style="text-align:center"|硫酸塩泉||rowspan="2"|SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |純硫酸塩泉||硫酸塩泉 |- |正苦味泉||マグネシウム－硫酸塩泉||Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |石膏泉||カルシウム－硫酸塩泉||Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |含食塩－芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－石膏泉||カルシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－正苦味泉||マグネシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |}
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン#電荷による種類|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]（SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>）のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 === 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧|高血圧症]]、[[浮腫|むくみ]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[熱海温泉]]（静岡県） * [[飯坂温泉]]（福島県） * [[玉造温泉]]（島根県） * [[水上温泉]]（群馬県）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉の泉質]] [[Category:硫酸塩|*りゆうさんえんせん]]

'''硫酸塩泉'''（りゅうさんえんせん）は、掲示用泉質名に基づく[[温泉]]の[[泉質]]の分類の一種。[[温泉#療養泉|療養泉]]のうち塩類泉に分類される。
== 概要 == [[アルカリ金属]]・[[アルカリ土類金属]]の[[硫酸塩]]を主成分としていることからこの名称が付いている。俗に薬効が高いと言われる。
== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。 {| class="wikitable" |+ 新旧泉質名の対応<ref>{{Cite web| |title=参考資料2新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> !旧泉質名!!新泉質名!!略記泉質名 |- |colspan="2" style="text-align:center"|硫酸塩泉||rowspan="2"|SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |純硫酸塩泉||硫酸塩泉 |- |正苦味泉||マグネシウム－硫酸塩泉||Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |石膏泉||カルシウム－硫酸塩泉||Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |含食塩－芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－石膏泉||カルシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－正苦味泉||マグネシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |}
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン#電荷による種類|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]（SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>）のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 === 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧|高血圧症]]、[[浮腫|浮腫]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[熱海温泉]]（静岡県） * [[飯坂温泉]]（福島県） * [[玉造温泉]]（島根県） * [[水上温泉]]（群馬県）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン (化学)#陰イオン|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]（SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>）のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 === 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧|高血圧症]]、[[浮腫]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[熱海温泉]]（静岡県） * [[飯坂温泉]]（福島県） * [[玉造温泉]]（島根県） * [[水上温泉]]（群馬県）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉の泉質]] [[Category:硫酸塩|*りゆうさんえんせん]]

revision.comment: Cite webにおける引数修正・アーカイブ追加もしくはテンプレート変更
revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|硫酸塩泉の例、[[玉造温泉]]]] '''硫酸塩泉'''（りゅうさんえんせん）は、掲示用泉質名に基づく[[温泉]]の[[泉質]]の分類の一種。[[温泉#療養泉|療養泉]]のうち塩類泉に分類される。
== 概要 == [[アルカリ金属]]・[[アルカリ土類金属]]の[[硫酸塩]]を主成分としていることからこの名称が付いている。俗に薬効が高いと言われる。
== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。 {| class="wikitable" |+ 新旧泉質名の対応<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=参考資料2新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> !旧泉質名!!新泉質名!!略記泉質名 |- |colspan="2" style="text-align:center"|硫酸塩泉||rowspan="2"|SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |純硫酸塩泉||硫酸塩泉 |- |正苦味泉||マグネシウム－硫酸塩泉||Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |石膏泉||カルシウム－硫酸塩泉||Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |含食塩－芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－石膏泉||カルシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－正苦味泉||マグネシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |}
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン (化学)#陰イオン|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]（SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>）のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 === 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧|高血圧症]]、[[浮腫]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[熱海温泉]]（静岡県） * [[飯坂温泉]]（福島県） * [[玉造温泉]]（島根県） * [[水上温泉]]（群馬県）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉の泉質]] [[Category:硫酸塩|*りゆうさんえんせん]]

revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|en|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|en|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|en|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|en|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|en|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|en|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|en|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|en|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|en|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|en|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|en|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Locations == * [[Atami Onsen]] (Shizuoka Prefecture) * [[Iizaka Onsen]] (Fukushima Prefecture) * [[Tamatsukuri Onsen]] (Shimane Prefecture) * [[Minakami Onsen]] (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}
[[Category:Onsen water qualities]] [[Category:Sulfates|*Sulfate Springs]] [[ja:硫酸塩泉]]

== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|en|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|en|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|en|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|en|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|en|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|en|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|en|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|en|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|en|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|en|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|en|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Locations == * [[Atami Onsen]] (Shizuoka Prefecture) * [[Iizaka Onsen]] (Fukushima Prefecture) * [[Tamatsukuri Onsen]] (Shimane Prefecture) * [[Minakami Onsen]] (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

[[Category:Onsen water qualities]] [[Category:Sulfates|*Sulfate Springs]] [[Category:Nnnnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:硫酸塩泉]]

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|en|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|en|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|en|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|en|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|en|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|en|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|en|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|en|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|en|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|en|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|en|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Locations == * [[Atami Onsen]] (Shizuoka Prefecture) * [[Iizaka Onsen]] (Fukushima Prefecture) * [[Tamatsukuri Onsen]] (Shimane Prefecture) * [[Minakami Onsen]] (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases

=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.

== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)

revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-qq]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-qq]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-qq]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Sulfate Spring]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Onsen water qualities]] [[Category:taggint in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:硫酸塩泉]]

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sulfate spring-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[category{Sulfate Spring]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:taggint in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sulfate spring]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:硫酸塩泉]]

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉}} category of saline springs based on labeling.
== Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal}}s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal}}s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症}}, these springs are said to help with: * {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis}} * Cuts and burns * Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis}} * {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone}} * {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity}} * {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus}} * {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout}} * Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症}}. * For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension}}, {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema}}, or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism}} in cases where the spring contains iodine.
== Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉}} (Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} (Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉}} (Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉}} (Gunma Prefecture)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}

revision.text:
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|硫酸塩泉の例、[[玉造温泉]]]] '''硫酸塩泉'''（りゅうさんえんせん）は、掲示用泉質名に基づく[[温泉]]の[[泉質]]の分類の一種。[[温泉#療養泉|療養泉]]のうち塩類泉に分類される。
== 概要 == [[アルカリ金属]]・[[アルカリ土類金属]]の[[硫酸塩]]を主成分としていることからこの名称が付いている。俗に薬効が高いと言われる。
== 新旧泉質名との対比 == 新旧泉質名では、以下に分類される。 {| class="wikitable" |+ 新旧泉質名の対応<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=参考資料2新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }}</ref> !旧泉質名!!新泉質名!!略記泉質名 |- |colspan="2" style="text-align:center"|硫酸塩泉||rowspan="2"|SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |純硫酸塩泉||硫酸塩泉 |- |正苦味泉||マグネシウム－硫酸塩泉||Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |石膏泉||カルシウム－硫酸塩泉||Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 |- |含食塩－芒硝泉||ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－石膏泉||カルシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |- |含食塩－正苦味泉||マグネシウム・ナトリウム－硫酸塩・塩化物泉||Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl泉 |}
== 泉質の定義 == 温泉水1kg中に含まれる含有成分が1,000mg以上あり、そのうち[[イオン (化学)#陰イオン|陰イオン]]の主成分が[[硫酸イオン]]（SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>）のもの。
== 効能 == ''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない''
泉質に基づく効能として、以下が挙げられる。 === 適応症 === ==== 浴用 ==== * [[一般的適応症]]のほか、[[動脈硬化症]]、[[切り傷]]、[[やけど]]、慢性[[皮膚病]]。
==== 飲用 ==== * 慢性[[胆嚢炎]]、[[胆石症]]、[[肥満|肥満症]]、[[糖尿病]]、[[痛風]]、慢性[[便秘]]。
=== 禁忌症 === * 浴用においては[[一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|一般的禁忌症]]。 * 飲用においては[[下痢]]のとき。 * 「ナトリウム－硫酸塩泉」においては、[[腎臓病]]や[[高血圧|高血圧症]]、[[浮腫]]があるとき、あるいは[[甲状腺機能亢進症]]のときに[[ヨウ素]]を含有する温泉の[[飲泉]]。
== 代表的な温泉地 == * [[熱海温泉]]（静岡県） * [[飯坂温泉]]（福島県） * [[玉造温泉]]（島根県） * [[水上温泉]]（群馬県）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:りゆうさんえんせん}} [[Category:温泉の泉質]] [[Category:硫酸塩|*りゆうさんえんせん]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:硫酸塩泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11587674]] Wikidata name: sulphur spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]]<nowiki/>water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Spring|qq=|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} category of saline springs based on labeling. == Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkali metal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline earth metal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]](Shizuoka Prefecture)
* [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]](Fukushima Prefecture)
* [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]](Shimane Prefecture)
* {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture) == Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

[[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]]
}}

Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]](Shizuoka Prefecture)
* [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]](Fukushima Prefecture)
* [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]](Shimane Prefecture)
* {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture) == Notes ==

[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]]<nowiki/>water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} category of saline springs based on labeling. == Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkali metal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline earth metal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.

== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]](Shizuoka Prefecture)
* [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]](Fukushima Prefecture)
* [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]](Shimane Prefecture)

Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]](Shizuoka Prefecture)
* [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]](Fukushima Prefecture)
* [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]](Shimane Prefecture)
* {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture) == Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:硫酸塩泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11587674]] Wikidata name: sulphur spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:140427 Chorakuen Tamatsukuri Onsen Matsue Shimane pref Japan15s3.jpg|thumb|right|Example of a sulfate spring ([[Tamatsukuri Onsen]])]] '''Sulfate Spring''' (硫酸塩泉, Ryūsan'en-sen) is a type of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]]<nowiki/>water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} category of saline springs based on labeling. == Overview == Sulfate springs are named for their primary component, the sulfate salts of {{ill|Alkali metal|ja|Alkali metal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkali metal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s and {{ill|Alkaline earth metal|ja|Alkaline earth metal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline earth metal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} s. These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:

{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]](Shizuoka Prefecture)
* [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]](Fukushima Prefecture)
* [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]](Shimane Prefecture)
* {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture) == Notes ==

These springs are popularly believed to have high medicinal benefits.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for sulfate springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Pure Sulfate Spring || Sulfate Spring || SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Bitter Spring || Magnesium-Sulfate Spring || Mg-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Glauber's Spring || Sodium-Sulfate Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Gypsum Spring || Calcium-Sulfate Spring || Ca-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring |- | Sodium-Chloride Sulfate Spring || Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Sodium-Calcium Sulfate Spring || Calcium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Ca･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |- | Magnesium-Sodium Sulfate Spring || Magnesium-Sodium-Sulfate-Chloride Spring || Mg･Na-SO<sub>4</sub>･Cl-Spring |}
== Definition == A sulfate spring is defined as a spring containing at least 1,000 mg of total dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) being the main anion.
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with sulfate springs:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, these springs are said to help with: ** {{ill|Atherosclerosis|ja|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** Cuts and burns ** Chronic skin diseases
==== Drinking ==== * Chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|ja|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gallstone|ja|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Obesity|ja|Obesity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obesity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Diabetes mellitus|ja|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diabetes mellitus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Gout|ja|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Chronic {{ill|Constipation|ja|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constipation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Contraindications === * For bathing: {{ill|General Contraindications|ja|一般的適応症#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}}
* For drinking: Not recommended during diarrhea. * For "Sodium-Sulfate Spring": Avoid drinking if you have {{ill|Kidney disease|ja|Kidney disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidney disease|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hypertension|ja|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , {{ill|Edema|ja|Edema|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edema|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , or {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|ja|Hyperthyroidism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyperthyroidism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}in cases where the spring contains iodine. == Representative Onsen Areas == * {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|WD=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen}}(Shizuoka Prefecture)
* {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|en|Iizaka Onsen|WD=Q17192714|lt=Iizaka Onsen}}(Fukushima Prefecture)
* {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|en|Tamatsukuri Onsen|WD=Q3514711|lt=Tamatsukuri Onsen}}(Shimane Prefecture)
* {{ill|Minakami Onsen|ja|水上温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture) == Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
{{Onsen}}
